title: AI Power Bottleneck — Gas Turbines and Nuclear Booked Solid
catalyst: Hyperscalers committed $630–700B in AI infrastructure capex for 2026, triggering an electricity demand surge that has sold out GE Vernova's gas turbine production slots through 2030. In Q1 2026, data center customers ordered more GE Vernova electrification equipment than in all of 2025 combined ($2.4B in a single quarter). The market is focused on chips and networking; the power infrastructure enabling them is less appreciated and increasingly scarce.
thesis: AI spending has a hidden critical path: every data center needs power, and the grid can't deliver at the required speed or scale. GE Vernova's gas turbine backlog hit 100 GW in Q1 2026 (up from 83 GW three months earlier), with slot reservations essentially sold out through 2030 — a structural constraint that gives GEV years of pricing power, evidenced by 10–20% price increases per kW on H1 2026 orders. Constellation Energy is the other side of this trade: as the nation's largest carbon-free power producer (60 GW after the Calpine acquisition), it holds the long-term PPAs that hyperscalers need to meet ESG commitments, with 2026 adjusted EPS guidance implying 49–63% growth at a reasonable 19.8x forward P/E.

ticker_rationale.GEV: GE Vernova is the dominant global gas turbine manufacturer with a backlog that expanded 20 GW in a single quarter to 100 GW — production is essentially sold out through 2030. New orders are being written at 10–20% higher prices per kW than Q4 2025. EBITDA margins are expanding from 8.4% (2025) toward 20% (2028) as higher-priced backlog flows through. At 43x forward P/E the stock is not cheap, but the visibility of a years-long sold-out order book makes it a rare durable earnings compounder in the industrial space.
ticker_rationale.CEG: Constellation Energy completed its $26.6B Calpine acquisition in January 2026, creating the nation's largest mixed nuclear-and-gas clean power portfolio at 60 GW. The 20-year Microsoft PPA for Crane Clean Energy Center (Three Mile Island restart) sets the template for hyperscaler clean-power contracts. At 19.8x forward P/E against 49–63% guided EPS growth in 2026 and a 20%+ base earnings growth trajectory through 2030, CEG offers the most attractive risk/reward in the AI power theme.

risks: AI training efficiency gains (distillation, inference optimization) could reduce compute and thus power demand faster than expected, undermining the order thesis
GEV's premium valuation (43x forward P/E) leaves no room for execution misses — any revenue shortfall vs. the $44–45B 2026 guide triggers sharp de-rating
GEV's wind segment continues bleeding (~$400M projected loss in 2026), which must be subsidized by gas and electrification profits
CEG's Calpine integration complexity and NRC delays on Three Mile Island restart could push out the Microsoft PPA contribution beyond 2028
A sustained US–Iran ceasefire and cheaper energy prices could reduce urgency of new power generation investment
summary: Hyperscaler AI capex of $700B in 2026 has created a power infrastructure bottleneck, with GE Vernova's gas turbine backlog sold out through 2030 at rising prices and Constellation Energy's 60 GW clean power fleet capturing premium hyperscaler PPAs.
body_md:
## Setup

The AI infrastructure buildout has exposed a critical bottleneck that most investors are overlooking: electricity. While the market obsesses over GPUs and networking, every data center requires massive power generation capacity that the existing grid cannot deliver at the required speed or scale. Hyperscalers collectively committed $630-700 billion in AI infrastructure capex for 2026 — more than Sweden's entire GDP — and a significant portion of that spending ultimately flows to power equipment and electricity procurement [1][2].

GE Vernova's Q1 2026 results laid bare the magnitude of this constraint. Gas turbine orders surged to 100 GW of combined backlog and slot reservations, up from 83 GW just three months earlier — a 20 GW expansion in a single quarter [3]. Data center customers alone ordered more GE Vernova electrification equipment in Q1 2026 than in all of 2025 combined. The company shipped 25 gas turbines in Q1 (up 32% YoY) with pricing rising faster than inflation, and management now expects at least 110 GW of combined backlog by year-end 2026 [3]. Production slots are essentially sold out through 2030, giving GEV years of pricing power and earnings visibility.

## Why Now

Three converging forces make this opportunity timely. First, the sheer scale of hyperscaler capex has overwhelmed the power equipment supply chain. Amazon leads with $200 billion in planned 2026 capex, Alphabet doubled its guidance to $175-185 billion, Meta raised its target to $135 billion, and Microsoft is on track for $120 billion+ [2]. Each new data center campus requires hundreds of megawatts of firm, dispatchable power — and gas turbines remain the fastest path to delivering that capacity.

Second, the regulatory timeline for new power generation is measured in years, not months. Permitting, interconnection queues, and construction timelines mean that power ordered today won't be delivered until 2028-2030. This creates a structural scarcity premium for companies that already hold production slots or own operating generation assets.

Third, the carbon-free power market is tightening as hyperscalers face ESG mandates. Constellation Energy's 20-year Microsoft PPA for the Crane Clean Energy Center (Three Mile Island restart) established the template: hyperscalers will pay premium prices for 24/7 carbon-free nuclear power to meet their sustainability commitments [4]. With the Calpine acquisition complete, Constellation now operates 60 GW of mixed nuclear and gas capacity — the largest clean energy portfolio in the nation.

## Company-Specific Edge

GE Vernova (GEV) is the dominant global gas turbine manufacturer with an installed base spanning 7,000+ gas turbines in over 100 countries. The Q1 2026 results were extraordinary: orders of $18.3 billion (+71% organically), $13 billion of sequential backlog growth, and total backlog reaching $150 billion [3]. The company raised 2026 revenue guidance to $44-45 billion with free cash flow of $5.0-5.5 billion. EBITDA margins are on an expansion trajectory from 8.4% in 2025 to 11-13% in 2026 and targeting 20% by 2028 as higher-priced orders flow through the P&L [5]. The balance sheet is clean: D/E of 9.7% with $8.8 billion in cash. The wind segment remains a drag (~$400M projected loss in 2026), but gas and electrification profits more than compensate.

Constellation Energy (CEG) offers the complementary long-duration play. Q1 2026 net income of $1.59 billion ($4.49 EPS) demonstrated the earnings power of the combined Calpine fleet, with adjusted operating EPS of $2.74 (up from $2.14 in Q1 2025) [4]. Management reaffirmed full-year 2026 adjusted operating earnings guidance of $11.00-$12.00 per share, including approximately $2 per share of Calpine accretion [4]. The base earnings growth trajectory of 20%+ through 2030 is underpinned by the nation's largest nuclear fleet, which provides the 24/7 carbon-free baseload power that hyperscalers demand. The Crane Clean Energy Center restart (targeted 2028) with a 20-year Microsoft PPA represents additional upside not fully priced in.

## Numbers That Matter

**GE Vernova (GEV)**:
- Backlog: 100 GW gas turbines (Q1 2026), targeting 110+ GW by year-end
- Revenue: $44-45B guided for 2026 (16-18% growth)
- Free cash flow: $5.0-5.5B guided for 2026
- Margin expansion: 8.4% EBITDA (2025) to 20% target (2028)
- Valuation: 43.3x forward P/E, D/E 9.7%, ROE 42.6%

**Constellation Energy (CEG)**:
- Capacity: ~60 GW post-Calpine (largest US clean energy producer)
- EPS guidance: $11.00-$12.00 adjusted (49-63% growth)
- Q1 2026: $2.74 adjusted operating EPS (+28% YoY)
- Valuation: 19.8x forward P/E, D/E 63.9%, ROE 16.4%
- Catalyst: Crane Clean Energy Center restart by 2028

## Bear Case

GEV's primary risk is valuation. At 43x forward P/E and 56x trailing, the stock prices in near-perfect execution of the margin expansion roadmap. Any revenue shortfall versus the $44-45B guide, or delays in the gas-to-electrification margin expansion, would trigger sharp de-rating. The wind segment's ~$400M projected loss in 2026 remains a persistent drag that must be subsidized by profitable segments.

For CEG, the Calpine integration is enormous — absorbing a $26.6 billion acquisition while managing the $1.6 billion Three Mile Island restart creates organizational complexity [4]. NRC regulatory delays on the Crane restart could push the Microsoft PPA contribution beyond 2028. Profit margin of 9.1% is thin for a company at this valuation premium, and the Calpine natural gas fleet introduces carbon intensity that may create ESG headwinds.

For both companies, AI training efficiency gains could reduce compute and power demand faster than expected. A sustained US-Iran ceasefire and normalization of energy prices could also reduce the urgency of new power generation investment.

## Conclusion

The AI power bottleneck thesis rests on a simple but powerful structural observation: $700 billion in AI capex requires enormous electricity generation capacity that does not exist today and takes years to build. GE Vernova owns the supply side with a sold-out gas turbine backlog and multi-year pricing power. Constellation Energy owns the demand side with the nation's largest clean power fleet and premium hyperscaler PPAs. Together, they provide complementary exposure to the AI power theme — GEV for margin expansion on a multi-year order book, CEG for earnings growth at a reasonable valuation. The key monitoring points are GEV's margin trajectory toward 20% EBITDA and CEG's ability to execute the Calpine integration while advancing the Crane restart.
invalidation: GE Vernova gas turbine backlog declines below 90 GW for two consecutive quarters, signaling order cancellations or demand destruction
Hyperscaler AI capex guidance is cut by more than 20% in aggregate, evidenced by two or more hyperscalers reducing their annual capex plans
Constellation Energy revenue or adjusted EPS guidance is lowered below $10.00 per share, signaling Calpine integration problems or demand weakness

sources.title: Big Tech's $700 billion AI spending spree has no clear end in sight

GE Vernova gas turbine backlog hits 100 GW as prices rise
Constellation Energy Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Doubles on Calpine, EPS Guidance Affirmed
GE Vernova reports first quarter 2026 financial results and raises 2026 guidance
